claim_boundary: Previously inspected development Entity outputs; not unseen evidence.

multi_mention_clusters.identity_rationale: All three mentions denote the gradient Concept as the vector of first-order partial derivatives used to describe local change; the calculus composition context and optimisation context do not change the object identity.
multi_mention_clusters.notes: Cross-lecture identity spanning two calculus lectures and one optimisation lecture.

multi_mention_clusters.identity_rationale: Both mentions denote a point at which the gradient is zero, at the same Concept granularity.
multi_mention_clusters.notes: Cross-lecture identity between calculus and optimisation.

reviewed_distinct_cases.rationale: The Formula is a specific first-order expression; the Concept is the broader Taylor approximation idea.
A composite function is the object differentiated; Chain Rule is a Method applied to it.
The Gradient Concept is distinct from the Multivariable Chain Rule Formula in which it appears.
The dot product is the standard coordinate instance of an inner product, not the same general Concept.
Gradient Descent is a Method; its update is a Formula that formalizes the Method.
Bayes' Rule and the conditional-probability definition are distinct Formula objects.
